title: Protecting Yourself from AI Surveillance

modules.title: Module 1: How AI-Powered Surveillance Works — Tracking, Profiling, and Inference at Scale
modules.sub: Understanding the mechanics behind modern AI-driven surveillance systems
modules.description: Learners explore how AI aggregates seemingly harmless data points to build detailed behavioral profiles. They examine real-world examples of inference at scale, developing a foundational mental model of how tracking actually works before applying any countermeasures.

modules.title: Module 2: Who Is Watching — Advertisers, Data Brokers, Employers, and State Actors
modules.sub: Identifying the key actors who collect and exploit your personal data
modules.description: Learners map the landscape of surveillance actors, from ad networks and data brokers to employers and government agencies, understanding each actor's motivations and methods. By the end, learners can identify which actors are most relevant to their own situation and begin shaping a personal threat model.

modules.title: Module 3: What Your Devices Expose — Sensors, Metadata, and Behavioral Fingerprints
modules.sub: Discovering what your phone, laptop, and apps silently reveal
modules.description: Learners audit the data surfaces their everyday devices create, including GPS signals, microphone access, metadata embedded in files, and behavioral fingerprints from typing patterns or browsing habits. They leave with a clear inventory of their highest-risk exposure points.

modules.title: Module 4: Practical Countermeasures — Settings, Tools, and Habits That Actually Work
modules.sub: Applying realistic, layered privacy protections without technical expertise
modules.description: Learners work through a prioritized checklist of device settings, browser configurations, app permissions, and privacy-respecting tool alternatives calibrated to their personal threat model. They practice making deliberate trade-offs between convenience and protection, selecting countermeasures they can realistically sustain.

modules.title: Module 5: When Surveillance Comes From People You Know — Stalkerware and Domestic Threats
modules.sub: Recognizing and responding to surveillance by intimate or known abusers
modules.description: Learners identify the signs of stalkerware and covert monitoring installed by partners, family members, or acquaintances, and understand why standard privacy advice must be adapted in these high-risk scenarios. They work through a safety-first decision framework for documenting and addressing these threats without inadvertently escalating danger.

modules.title: Module 6: Your Legal Rights — Data Deletion, Opt-Outs, and When to Escalate
modules.sub: Using privacy laws and formal processes to reclaim control of your data
modules.description: Learners navigate the legal tools available to them, including data deletion requests, opt-out rights under regulations like GDPR and CCPA, and formal complaint processes. They practice drafting a data subject access request and learn the specific indicators that signal when legal or law enforcement escalation is warranted.

modules.title: Module 7: Building a Sustainable Privacy Routine Without Paranoia
modules.sub: Turning one-time fixes into lasting, low-friction privacy habits
modules.description: Learners synthesize the course into a personalized, tiered privacy routine that fits their actual lifestyle, threat model, and tolerance for friction. They create a lightweight maintenance schedule and identify trusted resources for staying informed as surveillance technologies continue to evolve.

synopsis: AI has supercharged the ability of corporations, governments, and abusive individuals to track behavior, infer personal facts, and build detailed profiles from data most people assume is harmless. This course gives everyday users clear, actionable steps to reduce their surveillance exposure without requiring technical expertise or abandoning the tools they rely on.
rationale: AI-powered data aggregation and behavioral inference have made traditional privacy advice — clear your cookies, use incognito mode — dangerously insufficient against the current threat landscape.
learning_outcome: Learners can audit their primary devices and accounts for surveillance exposure, apply a layered set of countermeasures appropriate to their personal threat model, and know when to seek additional legal or technical help.
source_signals: Launch HN: Tinfoil (YC X25): Verifiable Privacy for Cloud AI
